key_insight: A graph-based spatio-temporal learning framework can model breast MRI treatment trajectories to better predict neoadjuvant chemotherapy response.
clinical_relevance: If validated, this approach could help identify patients more likely to achieve pathological complete response and support more personalized treatment planning.
method: Uses a graph neural network to capture relationships across multiple DCE-MRI timepoints.
Adds three self-supervised objectives to learn richer longitudinal trajectory representations.
Tests the impact of scan count and inter-scan time differences on prediction performance.
evidence_signal: Evaluated on 585 patients from the public ISPY-2 dataset, the method outperformed vision and self-supervised baselines across multiple classification metrics.
watch_item: Performance is reported on a single public cohort, so external validation and real-world generalizability remain important.
paper_title: Graph Representation Learning of Longitudinal Medical Imaging Trajectories for Treatment Response Prediction
